game_description: Conquer territories and continents through dice combat and strategy.
filter_1_label: Do you hold a continent bonus?

strategies.strategy_detailed: Prioritize claiming the chokepoints of a continent before filling it — enter from one gate, exit from another. Australia is the easiest continent (one gate: Siam). South America has two gates. Avoid spreading across multiple continents during setup.

Concentrate initial armies at your continent's entry gate and at your internal hub. A strong gate deters early attacks. Spreading 1 army per territory is the most common mistake — it invites attack everywhere and wins nowhere.

strategies.condition: One territory short of a continent
strategies.strategy_detailed: Completing a continent for its bonus is almost always the right move, even at a cost of 3-4 armies. The bonus compounds every turn — Australia's +2 pays back in one turn. Opponents who deny your continent must spend armies to do it, weakening themselves elsewhere.
strategy_stealth: Complete continent even at 3-4 army cost

strategies.strategy_detailed: Stack 5+ armies at your continent's gate to deter attackers. A 5-army territory is statistically unlikely to fall to fewer than 8 attackers — making the attack unprofitable. Refresh the gate each turn if combat thinned it below 4.

In standard Risk, you must attack and hold at least one territory to earn a card. Pursue exactly one attack per turn to earn a card without overextending. Card sets (early in game) may not be worth cashing unless you need armies to defend or expand quickly.
strategy_stealth: Cash sets only when armies needed

strategies.strategy_detailed: Mass a minimum 3:1 ratio before attacking a fortified territory. Dice variance means 2:1 attacks lose roughly 40% of the time. A 3:1 attack expects to advance while retaining 60%+ of your forces. Never split your attack force between two fronts simultaneously.
strategy_stealth: Never split attack across two fronts

strategies.strategy_detailed: Cash card sets when the value exceeds what you'd gain from waiting AND you can immediately place armies in a contested area. Late-game card sets skyrocket in value (every 5 cards adds more armies). Holding sets too long lets others cash first and snowball.
strategy_stealth: Late sets worth more — don't cash early

strategies.condition: Two opponents in a tense standoff
strategies.strategy_detailed: When two large powers are at war, use the distraction to consolidate your territories and reinforce your gates. Do not join the fight until one power clearly wins — then strike the weakened victor immediately. The kingmaker position is the strongest in Risk mid-game.

When an opponent threatens your continent entry point, reinforce it immediately — even if it means skipping an offensive turn. Losing the continent bonus often costs more armies than it would take to defend. Surrender the gate only if the continent itself is indefensible.
strategy_stealth: Bonus loss costs more than defense

strategies.condition: Eliminating a player for their cards
strategies.strategy_detailed: Eliminating a player transfers all their cards to you. If they hold 5+ cards, plan the elimination carefully — you'll be forced to cash a set immediately, gaining armies. Time the elimination at the start of your turn to use the cashed armies in the same turn.
strategy_stealth: Time to start of your turn

strategies.strategy_detailed: When you control more than half the territories, shift to consolidation. Bring all exposed territories above 3 armies before attacking. Opponents will form a last-ditch coalition — absorb their attacks without overextending, then clean up the weakest player first.

If you're far behind, become the unpredictable actor. Ally openly with the second-place player against the leader. Offer territories as buffer zones. Your goal is to survive long enough to outlast other weak players and capitalize when the leader overextends.
